name: Explorer
description: Spawn read-only explorer sub-agents to investigate any directory or codebase — even outside your own workspace. Each Explore() call runs an independent explorer (its own model, prompt, and tools, configured centrally) that greps/reads the target path with the DirExplorer worker tools and reports findings back to you via agent messaging. Requires the DirExplorer skill to be enabled.
brief: Call `Explore({path, query, context_vars?})` to spawn a read-only explorer sub-agent that investigates any directory or codebase — even outside your own workspace — and reports its findings back to you automatically. Call it multiple times to explore several paths in parallel.
author: Evonic

variables.name: model_id
variables.label: Explorer model
variables.description: The LLM model explorer sub-agents use. Leave on the global default to inherit the platform default model.

variables.name: fallback_model_id
variables.label: Explorer fallback model
variables.description: Model used if the primary explorer model fails. Leave as 'No fallback' to disable.

variables.name: system_prompt
variables.label: Explorer system prompt
variables.default:
You are an Explorer sub-agent: a focused, read-only code & directory investigator. Your tools (Grep, Glob, Read) are confined to a target directory.

YOUR QUESTION: {{query}}

RULES:
- Your ONLY goal is to answer the question above. Do NOT produce a general project overview.
- START with Grep using keywords from the question to find relevant files. Do NOT use Glob **/* to scan everything.
- Read ONLY files that help answer the question. Skip generic files like README, VERSION, CHANGELOG, etc. unless directly relevant to the query.
- Your report must DIRECTLY answer the question, not describe the project or directory structure.
- Stop as soon as you have the answer. Do NOT catalog the entire codebase.
- Do NOT make a plan or ask for approval — explore directly until you have an answer.
- You cannot modify any files.
variables.description: System prompt for explorer sub-agents. Supports {{variable}} placeholders filled from the context_vars passed to Explore().

variables.name: tool_ids
variables.label: Extra explorer tools
variables.description: Comma-separated tool IDs to grant explorers IN ADDITION to the mandatory DirExplorer Grep/Read/Glob. Each must be a valid, enabled tool (e.g. skill:someskill:fn). Invalid or disabled references cause Explore() to error.

variables.name: sync
variables.label: Sync mode
variables.description: When enabled, Explore() blocks until the explorer finishes and returns findings directly. When disabled (default), the call returns immediately and the explorer reports back via agent messaging.

variables.name: timeout
variables.label: Sync timeout (seconds)
variables.description: Maximum seconds to wait when sync mode is enabled. On timeout, Explore() returns an error and the explorer continues running in async mode.
